title: Human Anatomical Substances
description: Does the project involve any of the following?

has_default_no.title: No - All responses below will be defaulted to NO

human_has_q1.title: 1. Collecting human specimens prospectively for research purposes

human_has_q2.title: 2. Human cell lines that cannot be purchased from a vendor
human_has_q2.description: This includes human cell lines that may be published or otherwise freely available, but are not commercially available

human_has_q3.title: 3. Commercially available human anatomical substances (non-pooled)
human_has_q3.description: Example: Tissue samples, PBMCs, primary cells, blood, saliva, etc. from a single donor

human_has_q4.title: 4. Creation of new patient-derived xenograft (PDX) models from human tissue samples

human_has_q5.title: 5. Using human specimens obtained through clinical trials, research studies, collaborations, biobanks, or clinical departments
human_has_q5.description: Includes: clinical trials or research studies, including existing publicly available specimen banks; collaboration with other investigators or institutions; established institutional clinical repositories, biobanks, or tumor banks; medical facility clinical departments/services (e.g., excess or discarded surgical materials)

human_has_q7.title: 7. Unique or regulated sample types, e.g., human embryonic stem cell lines
human_has_q7.description: Exception: Use of HEK293 cells does NOT require OHRO review
